claims.claim_text: Gemma 4 is presented as a current Google-backed open-model family.
claims.span_hint: section: What Gemma 4 Represents In This Module, first paragraph
claims.rationale: The Gemma 4 model card explicitly identifies Gemma as a Google DeepMind family of open models.

claims.claim_text: Llama is described as a broad reference point because many open-model tools, examples, and workflows are built around it.
claims.span_hint: section: Comparing Gemma 4 With Other Open Families, second paragraph
claims.rationale: The official Llama hub is a strong ecosystem anchor, but it does not directly prove the broad 'many tools, examples, and workflows' phrasing.

claims.claim_text: Qwen is described as especially relevant for multilingual work, coding interest, and strong open-model experimentation.
claims.span_hint: section: Comparing Gemma 4 With Other Open Families, second paragraph
claims.rationale: The Qwen3 model card directly highlights multilingual support plus reasoning, coding, and agent capabilities.

claims.claim_text: Mistral is described as relevant when efficiency, deployment shape, and quality-per-size are central concerns.
claims.span_hint: section: Comparing Gemma 4 With Other Open Families, second paragraph
claims.rationale: Mistral's release page directly supports scalable efficiency and deployment-range claims, but not the exact 'quality-per-size' wording.

claims.claim_text: MLX is an Apple-Silicon-oriented path for local experimentation.
claims.span_hint: section: Runtime Choice Changes The Evaluation, second paragraph
claims.rationale: The MLX project describes itself as machine learning infrastructure for Apple silicon.

claims.claim_text: Gemma 4 is attractive in the worked example because it has current ecosystem relevance and documented paths.
claims.span_hint: section: Worked Example: Choosing A Model Family For A Local RAG Lab, third paragraph
claims.rationale: The model card clearly shows documentation and integration paths, but 'current ecosystem relevance' is still a summarized judgment rather than a direct statement on the page.

claims.claim_text: Qwen may be a better fit when multilingual behavior or coding-focused experimentation is central.
claims.span_hint: section: When Another Family May Be A Better Fit, first paragraph
claims.rationale: The Qwen3 model card directly supports multilingual, reasoning, coding, and agent-oriented positioning.

claims.claim_text: Mistral may be a better fit when compactness, efficiency, or serving footprint dominate the decision.
claims.span_hint: section: When Another Family May Be A Better Fit, first paragraph
claims.rationale: Mistral's release materials support scalable efficiency and edge-to-enterprise deployment, but not the exact serving-footprint phrasing.

claims.claim_text: Open-model families are not single artifacts; they often include multiple sizes, variants, instruction-tuned releases, and quantized or runtime-specific builds.
claims.rationale: The Qwen3 model page is a solid anchor because it shows family variants and quantization/runtime paths, but the claim is generalized across open-model families.

further_reading.title: Gemma 4 E2B model card
further_reading.why_relevant: This is the primary source for the module's Gemma case-study claims, including variants, context windows, modalities, and integration paths.

further_reading.title: Meta Llama family hub
further_reading.why_relevant: It gives learners a direct family-level comparison point when mapping Gemma against another major open-model ecosystem.

notes: This module is mostly pedagogical. The citation-bearing claims are the family/runtime characterization sentences, especially where the prose moves from teaching method into assertions about Gemma, Llama, Qwen, Mistral, MLX, or vLLM. No hard-cite incident, pricing, benchmark, or security anecdotes were found.
